attack_surface: artifact package has unexpected top-level directories
closure_rule: top-level directory set equals artifact,paper

attack_surface: package path exposes process/meta residue
closure_rule: no forbidden process/meta path token appears outside the IEEE template internals

attack_surface: package contains transient LaTeX/Python/cache material
closure_rule: no cache, bytecode, or LaTeX transient files are present

attack_surface: delivery zip includes deterministic generated fixture by-products
closure_rule: generated fixtures are regenerated by replay and excluded from artifact integrity

attack_surface: paper-visible claims drift from executable ledgers
closure_rule: paper_claims, claim_manifest, and paper claim macros agree without depending on a pre-existing claim_verification.json

attack_surface: paper-visible numeric claims lack traceable evidence files
closure_rule: claim traceability gate passes and covers at least thirty paper-visible claims

attack_surface: source corpus provenance can be misread as all-upstream benchmark evidence
closure_rule: source-provenance gate explicitly separates official/upstream/native/stress/generated strata

attack_surface: adapter wrappers pass invariants without matching reference semantics
closure_rule: every counted adapter before/after decision agrees with executable reference semantics

attack_surface: artifact integrity manifest is stale or includes excluded generated material
closure_rule: artifact_integrity_check and artifact_manifest agree and exclude fixture by-products

attack_surface: bibliography contains uncited, fabricated, or untraceable entries
closure_rule: reference-integrity gate covers all cited entries and reports zero failures

attack_surface: PDF violates 10-page body plus 2-page references shape
closure_rule: main.pdf has 12 pages, supplement.pdf is a substantive companion, and page 11 begins the reference region

attack_surface: anonymous artifact hygiene is not part of the artifact hygiene check
closure_rule: hygiene verifier is present and re-run in the clean reproducibility protocol

attack_surface: runtime dependencies are resolved from reviewer workstation rather than vendored/pinned artifact material
closure_rule: dependency provenance gate proves exact pins, dist-info metadata, and vendor import resolution

attack_surface: unexercised Python modules contain syntax or import breakage
closure_rule: module import gate compiles all first-class Python files and imports all latticeguard modules

attack_surface: primary denominator mixes applicable rows with rejected, unsupported, errors, timeouts, baselines, or seeded controls
closure_rule: denominator integrity gate independently checks accounting across obligations, rejections, coverage, baselines, and exclusions

attack_surface: released text files contain local paths, instruction/procedure residue, or stale process-oriented terms
closure_rule: content-residue gate reports zero findings over first-class text files

attack_surface: external resources or vendored dependencies retain unresolved license/provenance disposition
closure_rule: resource-license gate checks third-party notices, vendored license files, and closed external-resource dispositions

attack_surface: first-class Python files import undeclared reviewer-environment packages
closure_rule: import-surface gate statically checks first-class Python files and permits only stdlib, local modules, and declared vendored dependencies

attack_surface: README or reproduction prose retains stale paper-visible counts after final edits
closure_rule: narrative claim scan gate checks key README/reproduction counts against executable ledgers

attack_surface: main manuscript reads like a file/function checklist or leaks instruction/process residue
closure_rule: manuscript-presentation gate checks main-paper prose for file names, code-font identifiers, instruction residue, and over-engineered checklist language

attack_surface: submission misses Data Availability or required non-identifying tool-use disclosure
closure_rule: open-science compliance gate checks main-paper Data Availability and supplemental tool-use disclosure without identity leakage
